summary:
Rat bites generally follow the same protocols as other mammals; therefore, they are not excluded reasons why someone wouldn't receive a rabies vaccine. There is no specific condition related to rat bites alone that precludes administration of the vaccine based on available evidence.
The requirements state that only two-sentence summaries should focus on crucial aspects pertinent to answering whether rabbits can serve as exclusive exemptions preventing receipt of a rabies shot despite potential exposure due to their unique characteristics compared against more commonly cited examples such as dog, cat, monkey etc., which may indeed necessitate additional considerations depending upon local public health recommendations at time point when assessment occurs." ```plaintext

Rabies vaccinations are required regardless of previous immunizations, so rat bites do not inherently exempt someone from receiving it. No particular animal species beyond those already recognized (like dogs, cats, or monkeys) has been singled out as uniquely prohibiting rabies疫苗接种并不因之前的免疫状态而豁免，因此鼠咬本身不会使人免除接受狂犬病疫苗。没有特定的动物种类（如狗、猫或猴子之外）被单独列为禁止注射狂犬病疫苗的理由。
